raw_consumer_statement: In XX/XX/XXXX there were several charges on my XXXX Citibank card that were not mine. I contacted the company and they removed them. The next month on my XXXX bill there were also multiple charges for clothing in XXXX and Washington. I attempted to contact Citibank but was told since I could not provide my previous phone and email account they could not verify my identity. They did not recognize my new one. I had to change it because it was hacked. They were to send an attestation form via mail for me to complete. I asked them to please freeze the account until it was resolved. When I had not received the form by XX/XX/XXXX I called back and they said they had sent it and it was probably caught up in holiday mail. I asked for another and asked once again that they freeze the account. I just receive my Bill for XXXX charges and there are an additional {$320.00} in charges that made on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX I did not make. I am XXXX XXXX XXXX and getting very frustrated by the roadblocks they seem to be putting up. I even have problems understanding the Citibank staff because of the thick accents. This is an especially difficult issue for seniors with any hearing loss. At this point I would just want to be able to get help in getting the charges reversed and the card replaced. It is more than clear it has been hacked.
